data.reasoningText:
Now I understand the project. Let me migrate from xUnit.net v2 to v3:

1. **TestProject.csproj**: 
   - Replace `xunit` v2.9.3 with `xunit.v3` (latest v3 package)
   - Replace `YTest.MTP.XUnit2` with `YTest.MTP.XUnit3` (the MTP adapter for v3)
   
2. **BasicTests.cs**: 
   - In xUnit v3, `using Xunit;` stays the same
   - `[Fact]` and `Assert` are still the same
   - The core test attributes and assertions remain unchanged, though there may be some behavioral differences under the hood
   - For xUnit v3, the main package is `xunit.v3` which replaces the older `xunit` package
   - xUnit v3 has built-in Microsoft.Testing.Platform support, so we likely won't need the separate `YTest.MTP.XUnit2` adapter anymore
   - I should update the package references to `xunit.v3` and switch to `YTest.MTP.XUnit3` as the counterpart adapter
   - The test code itself should mostly work as-is since `[Fact]` and `Assert.True()` are still supported, though I may need to check if any namespaces have changed in v3

, and for the MTP adapter I'd need to use `YTest.MTP.XUnit3` as the corresponding package. Let me build and test this to make sure the migration actually works.

data.content: xUnit v3 has built-in MTP support, so the separate adapter isn't needed. Let me also check available versions and adjust:
data.reasoningText:
The `YTest.MTP.XUnit3` package doesn't exist. Let me check what xUnit v3 needs for Microsoft.Testing.Platform integration. 

In xUnit v3, MTP support is built-in. The `xunit.v3` meta-package includes `xunit.runner.inproc.console` which provides the MTP runner. So we likely don't need the `YTest.MTP.XUnit3` adapter at all.

Actually wait, let me check if `YTest.MTP.XUnit2` is even a real package... it might be a custom/internal package. Let me check NuGet.
 
Looking at this more carefully, xUnit v3 has the runner built directly into the test project itself. The key difference is that the test project needs to be configured as an executable with `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>`, since xUnit v3 uses an in-process runner and generates its own entry point. This means we don't need a separate test runner adapter at all—the migration just requires updating from the `xunit` package to `xunit.v3` and adjusting the project configuration.

Since xUnit v3 includes built-in MTP support, we can remove the `YTest.MTP.XUnit2` reference entirely. Let me verify what the latest version of `xunit.v3` is to make sure we're targeting the right package version.

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "dotnet package search xunit.v3 --exact-match 2>&1 | head -20", "description": "Search for xunit.v3 package", "initial_wait": 15}
